headline: First-ever evidence-based baseline: dual-trigger Gen-2 cluster invalidation — WARN Act violation investigation (163 workers, April 7) + reinstated racial harassment class action (Davis v. General Mills, N.D. Ga., April 22 'one more chance' ruling)
methodology_notes: F500 Gen-2 cluster 4×7+3.5×1=81.4 — fifth consecutive forensic anchor (TIAA, Masimo, BD, Danaher, General Mills). Dual-trigger profile (EMP + EQU) makes this the second-strongest Night-7 anchor after Danaher's securities settlement. Differentiated dimension shape vs. Danaher: EMP lowered to 50.0 (WARN Act notice failure for 163 workers; layoff scheduled June 8 with notice on April 2-6 — does not satisfy 60-day WARN requirement) and EQU lowered to 43.8 (decades-of-racial-harassment class action; federal judge characterized plant leadership as 'Good Ole Boy network'; case survived dismissal motion in April 2026). SYS 62.5 retained on global food access mission floor. INT 50.0 reflects two parallel governance failure patterns (worker notice + race discrimination) without documented executive falsification, distinguishing from Danaher securities fraud. Band change Exemplary → Functional consistent with BD calibration.

evidence.claim: WARN Act investigation: General Mills filed notice April 2, 2026 of plant closure at St. Charles, MO eliminating 163 jobs effective on or about June 8 — Strauss Borrelli investigation alleges this fails 60-day statutory notice requirement
163 workers at St. Charles MO pizza-crust facility laid off

Davis v. General Mills Operations, LLC (N.D. Ga. case 1:24-cv-02409) — Black workers allege decades of racial slurs, demotion, and harassment by 'Good Ole Boy' network at Covington/Atlanta-area cereal/trail-mix plant; April 22, 2026 federal court ruling gave plaintiffs 'one more chance' to perfect class-action pleadings rather than dismissing for good
Independent reporting on Davis class-action allegations (CBS News, NPR, Today, HR Dive, Unicorn Riot)
Civil Rights Litigation Clearinghouse case docket for Davis v. General Mills
Published 81.4 is uniform 4×7+3.5×1 Gen-2 cluster profile invalidated by BD forensic anchor (April 24)

key_evidence: WARN Act violation investigation: 163-worker layoff at St. Charles MO with 60-day-notice failure allegation (April 2-7, 2026)
Davis v. General Mills (N.D. Ga.) racial-harassment class action survived dismissal April 22, 2026 — 'Good Ole Boy network' allegations at Georgia cereal/trail-mix plant
Dual independent triggers in EMP and EQU dimensions distinguish General Mills from single-trigger Gen-2 anchors
EMP 50.0 and EQU 43.8 are the lowest dimension scores; SYS 62.5 retained on food-access mission floor
Published 81.4 is uniform Gen-2 cluster profile — fifth consecutive forensic invalidation
